name: Laparoscopic appendectomy

figure_scopes.overview: Cecum, terminal ileum, and appendix orientation in the right iliac fossa
figure_scopes.storyboard: Orientation -> mesoappendix/artery -> base control -> specimen removed
figure_scopes.closeup: Appendiceal base ligation/staple line at the cecum

laterality: Right lower quadrant (right iliac fossa); appendiceal base at the cecum where the taeniae coli converge
must_have: Cecum, terminal ileum, appendix, appendiceal base, mesoappendix, and appendiceal artery.
Appendiceal base connected to the cecum near the convergence of taeniae coli.
Mesoappendix attached to the appendix with vascular supply shown coherently.
spatial_rules: The appendix may vary in direction, but its base must remain fixed at the cecum.
Terminal ileum should connect to the cecum and should not be mistaken for the appendix.
Appendiceal artery travels in the mesoappendix rather than directly from a large central trunk.

forbidden_errors.error: A free-floating appendix detached from the cecum.
Terminal ileum confused with the appendix.
Appendiceal artery shown arising directly from the aorta.

safety_concept: Secure the appendiceal base at the cecum and identify the appendiceal artery in the mesoappendix before division.
phases: Cecum, terminal ileum, and appendix orientation.
Base control / ligation or staple line at the appendiceal base.
Removed appendix with cecum preserved, if a final panel is needed.
verify: Appendiceal base remains attached to the cecum in orientation panels.
Terminal ileum and appendix are visually distinct.
Mesoappendix anatomy is present when vessels are shown.
